title: Chains We Choose
productionNotes: Open with sparse hand drums (djembe and shakers) establishing hypnotic 85 BPM foundation. Bass drops heavy and dubby at verse 1, analog synth stabs punctuate frustration. Live percussion throughout—congas, bongos, tambourine—building intensity each section. Chorus needs hard-hitting 808 kicks with reverb tail, creating physical weight. Bridge strips to voice and hand drums only before final explosive chorus with added horn stabs (trombone/trumpet) and doubled vocal aggression. Mix the patois authentically raw, slight delay on key phrases.
intro:
Nyabinghi drums, phone notification sounds layered rhythmically
Spoken word over percussion: 'Dem nuh chain we with iron no more...we pick up di shackle weself'
verse1:
Wake up inna di mornin', first ting mi check phone screen
Scrollin' through di highlight reel, chasin' what dem want mi fi be
Buyin' tings mi cyan't afford, jus' fi stunt pon Instagram
Workin' three job fi one chain, tell mi who's di free man?
Dem sell wi dream inna package, we sign up with wi own hand
Credit card slavery, dem call it 'opportunity' inna Babylon land
Youth dem killin' one another over sneaker and chain
While di system sit back and profit off di pain
prechorus:
Open up yuh eye now, see di trap dem set
We build di cage, we turn di key, we livin' in wi own debt
chorus:
Chains we choose, chains we choose
No massa crackin' whip, but we still cyan't move
Gold pon wi neck while di mind stay enslaved
Chains we choose, chains we choose, oh Jah
Swipe left, swipe right, sell wi soul fi di views
Dese are di chains we choose
verse2:
Dem give wi forty brand, tell wi dat's identity
Consume until yuh empty, dat's di new captivity
Likes and validation, di new plantation we work pon
Algorithm massa, keep wi fingers scrollin' from dusk to dawn
Mi see mi sistren bleedin' her savings fi look like di model
Mi see mi bredren trapped inna bottle after bottle
We worship at di altar of di things we accumulate
Meanwhile Babylon laugh while we self-eliminate
bridge:
But wait... mi feel somethin' stirrin' inna mi chest
Like ancestral voice tellin' mi fi resist
Marcus say 'Emancipate yourself from mental slavery'
Di first step is seein' di chains clearly
Seein' dem clearly... clearly now
Mi see dem now
outro:
Hand drums intensify, building tension
'Di recognition is di first step to liberation...'
Drums and bass surge then sudden cut to silence with single heartbeat
promptForSuno: Conscious Dancehall, 85 BPM, D minor, deep warm Jamaican baritone male vocals with authentic patois, Nyabinghi hand drums, live percussion, heavy dub bassline, analog synth stabs, reflective and frustrated mood building to tension, roots reggae influence, 808 kicks in chorus, horn stabs in final section, reverb and delay on vocals, phone notification sounds as percussion in intro, Marcus Garvey inspired, anti-consumerism anthem, spiritual awakening themes
